overview: The Marathon County Board of Supervisors adopted the 2026 Comprehensive Plan with multiple amendments addressing renewable energy terminology, data centers, AI technology, and energy policy. The board also approved salaries for elected officials, authorized phase 2 design for a new highway facility, engaged outside counsel for PFAS litigation, and ratified a local emergency declaration for the recent blizzard.

agenda.item: Call to order, pledge of allegiance, and moment of reflection
Consent agenda items C8 through C13 B2
D14 - Adopting Marathon County Comprehensive Plan 2026, Ordinance O-13-26
E15 - Establishing salaries for clerk of courts, sheriff, and elected department heads
E16 - Authorizing phase 2 design services for new highway facility
E17 - Authorizing outside counsel for PFAS litigation
E18-E19 - Budget carryforwards and capital asset threshold resolutions
F20 - Law enforcement drug trafficking response grant
G21 - Ratification of local state of emergency declaration

discussions.body: Administrator Leonard presented 10 proposed amendments compiled from supervisor feedback. Amendment 1 (livability standards) passed unanimously. Amendments 2, 3, and 4 regarding alternative energy systems terminology were separated at Supervisor Crawl's request and each passed but not unanimously. Amendment 5 (data centers and battery storage) and Amendment 7 (regulate when allowed by law) both passed but not unanimously, with Supervisor Leur voting no on Amendment 5 citing it was 'too ideological.' Amendment 6 (radon and lead) passed unanimously. Amendment 8 (AI and automation language) proposed by Supervisor Leur passed unanimously. Amendment 9 by Supervisor Sundowski on coal, natural gas, and nuclear energy was amended by Supervisor Boots to read 'promote coal and natural gas until a long-term sustainable and reliable energy source can be found that does not adversely affect agricultural land' and passed but not unanimously. A late amendment by Supervisor Sundowski regarding utility-scale wind, solar, and battery storage as industrial uses was debated; Supervisor Jeang moved to refer it to committee but this was defeated, and the amendment itself was defeated. The final comprehensive plan as amended carried but was not unanimous.

Resolution 12-26 establishing salaries for the clerk of courts, sheriff, and elected department heads for the upcoming term was approved. Motion by Supervisor Conway, second by Supervisor Rosenberg. The motion carried.

Resolution 13-26 authorizing staff to proceed with phase 2 design services for the new highway facility was approved unanimously. Supervisor Soyber requested future information on plans for the old facility. Chair Gibbs noted that HR Finance and Property Committee will address this but it may be years before the transition occurs.

Resolution 14-26 authorizing engagement of outside counsel on a contingency basis for PFAS lawsuits passed unanimously with two amendments. Supervisor Robinson's amendment directing the county administrator to evaluate past and present practices that may have resulted in PFAS exposure passed unanimously. Vice Chair Dickinson's amendment modifying language regarding airport property also passed unanimously.

Resolution 22-26 ratifying the declaration of local state of emergency related to the recent blizzard passed unanimously. Administrator Leonard explained the ratification preserves potential reimbursement opportunities and praised county staff across multiple departments who worked 12-16 hour shifts, with some sleeping on cots to maintain operations. Supervisor Fifer echoed the praise as infrastructure committee chair.

The board accepted the executive committee's recommendation regarding the administrator's performance evaluation and salary placement under the new pay structure. Motion by Supervisor Robinson, second by Supervisor Cavelli. Chair Gibbs noted no wording changes were made from the summary reviewed the previous Thursday. The motion carried unanimously.

actionItems: Comprehensive Plan 2026 adopted as amended with Ordinance O-13-26
Salaries established for clerk of courts, sheriff, and elected department heads for upcoming term
Phase 2 design services for new highway facility authorized
Outside counsel engaged on contingency basis for PFAS litigation
County administrator directed to evaluate past and present practices regarding PFAS exposure
Budget carryforwards and associated amendments approved
Capital asset threshold set at $10,000 for general assets and $50,000 for infrastructure assets
Law enforcement drug trafficking response grant accepted with budget amendment
Local state of emergency declaration ratified
County administrator performance evaluation finalized with salary placement approved
Recognition of departing supervisors: Crawl, Fifick, Marshall, Rosenberg, Hardinger, V, and Reynolds
50th anniversary celebration of Hmong community in Wausau announced for April 4th at Central Wisconsin Expo Center
title: Marathon County Board Regular Meeting - 3/24/2026 - 3/24/2026
